block.electroenergetics.concrete_pole.to: Contains _Electrical Connectors_ on the top and bottom block of the _Pole_.
block.electroenergetics.emergency_stop_b: Changes state into _Engaged_, and opens its _Internal Switch_.
block.electroenergetics.indicator_bulb.t: While not useful for lighting, but can be used as an _Indicator_ or _Signal_.
death.attack.electroenergetics.hv_electr: %1$s was thrown into a High Voltage wire by %2$s
electroenergetics.ponder.alternator.head: Setting up an alternator
electroenergetics.ponder.alternator.text: Alternators can be used to convert rotational force to electricity
The alternator will start inducing a voltage.
Use a Transformer to fine-tune the voltage.
You might want to use a Voltage Regulator to regulate the voltage even further.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.header: An intuitive approach to understanding electricity
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_1: Imagine a wire connected in a loop.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_10: In this case, this movement heats up the bulb's filament, the heat makes it glow.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_11: Notice how the number of incoming dots is equal to the number of outgoing dots.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_12: This is the definition of Kirchhoff's Current Law.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_2: Little dots are placed on this wire. Think of them as electrons.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_3: Its like a chain. These dots can move, but they can't move away from each other.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_4: Voltage sources try to move this chain. They try to pull from one side, and push to the other.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_5: Notice how the number of these dots stay constant. These dots don't represent energy themselves, their movement is energy.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_6: This is actually a short circuit.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_7: There is no resistance, and the voltage source is able to move these dots really fast!
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_8: Connect it through a device instead.
electroenergetics.ponder.basics.text_9: Devices use the movement of these dots as energy.
electroenergetics.ponder.connectors.head: Connecting Wires
electroenergetics.ponder.connectors.text: Connectors and Wires can be used to relay electricity.
To remove existing wires, use an empty spool.
electroenergetics.ponder.connectors_chun: Electricity stays functional outside of loaded chunks.
electroenergetics.ponder.diodes.text_1: Diodes can be used to control the direction of current
electroenergetics.ponder.diodes.text_2: Diodes will allow current to flow, if the anode is connected to the higher potential ...
electroenergetics.ponder.diodes.text_3: ... and the cathode is connected to the lower potential
electroenergetics.ponder.diodes.text_5: Otherwise, the diode blocks current flow
electroenergetics.ponder.electric_train_: Electric trains emit a different sound when powered
Attaching certain blocks can change this sound to a different style
electroenergetics.ponder.energy_meter.te: Energy meters can be used to measure used energy
Connect the positive feed to the 'feed' node
Connect the positive load to the 'load' node
Finally, connect the return through the 'neutral' node
The meter will start counting energy that flowed through it
electroenergetics.ponder.grounding.heade: The ground as a conductor of electricity
electroenergetics.ponder.grounding.text_: The ground can actually conduct electricity.
electroenergetics.ponder.hv_switch.heade: Setting up a High Voltage Switch
electroenergetics.ponder.hv_switch.text_: High Voltage Switches can be used to connect or disconnect high voltage circuits
To create one, place a Connector in the direction of the High Voltage Switch
Leave a one-block gap between them
The High Voltage Switch can be operated manually...
electroenergetics.ponder.railway_electri: Catenary Holders will automatically connect to nearby pole-like blocks
A catenary system can be used to power trains
A train, that uses electricity, must have a pantograph that touches the catenary wires ...
To power the Catenary System, connect the overhead lines to the positive terminal
Connect the negative to a Ground Rod
electroenergetics.ponder.relay.text_1: Relays are just switches that can are operated with a small electric current
electroenergetics.ponder.relay.text_2: Connect the controlling circuit to the 'coil' connectors
electroenergetics.ponder.relay.text_3: Connect the switched circuit to the remaining connectors
electroenergetics.ponder.relay.text_4: Wrench the relay to change its state to Normally-Closed (NC)
electroenergetics.ponder.resistance.head: Effect of a Resistor on a Circuit
electroenergetics.ponder.resistance.text: Without a resistor, the bulb shines at full intensity
Notice how the bulb is dimmer
The resistor is limiting the current flowing through the bulb
The downside is, some power is wasted as heat
electroenergetics.ponder.switch.text_1: Switches can be used to interrupt the flow of current
electroenergetics.ponder.tag.electric_tr: These blocks change electric train sounds.
electroenergetics.ponder.third_rail.head: Using third rail
electroenergetics.ponder.third_rail.text: Electric trains can collect power from a third rail
For that, use a Rail Contact Shoe
electroenergetics.ponder.transformer.tex: Transformers can be used to change voltage.
electroenergetics.ponder.transformer_cor: Transformer cores can be configured with a specific number of turns.
Surrounding transformer core with radiator panels or waterlogging them increases its power rating.
Exceeding the transformer's power rating will cause it to overload.
electroenergetics.ponder.transformer_los: Wires have some resistance, therefore they cause energy losses.
Energy losses are directly proportional to the resistance and the square of the amperage.
Transformers can be used to increase the voltage, therefore decrease the amperage.
For large transmission lines, stepping-up the voltage can greatly decrease energy losses.
electroenergetics.ponder.transformer_tur: Transformers are used to step-up or step-down voltage
Notice how the turns ratio is equal to the voltage ratio
Additionally, the transformer doesn't connect the both circuits
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_1: Voltage is the measurement of difference of electric potential
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_10: Connect a device to a voltage difference, and current will start flowing and doing work
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_12: When two different potentials are connected with low resistance, a lot of current starts flowing
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_5: Sometimes a voltage value may describe a single point. In that case, it is a value relative to ground, which is always 0V
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_8: Notice how the voltage at each connector is -160V and 70V, but the difference is 230V
electroenergetics.ponder.voltage.text_9: Voltage doesn't mean work. It shows how much work could be done, it's not current or flow, just the possibility of it
item.electroenergetics.clamp_meter.toolt: Displays the _Amperage_ and _Direction_ of the _Current_ flowing through the _Wire_.
When R-Clicked while Hovering over a Wire
Measured the _Amperage_ of the _Current_ flowing through a _Wire_.
